definition: The EM demographic dividend has an unconditional realization rate of ~55-65%, rising to 80-85% with above-median institutional quality. The dividend is a self-destructing call option: income growth kills fertility once countries cross the $8K-12K GDP per capita threshold, unwinding the dividend precisely as it materializes.
evidence: Of ~30 countries with comparable demographic transitions (1960-2000), ~17-19 achieved growth acceleration (55-65%)
Conditional rate with above-median institutional quality: 80-85%
Income growth kills fertility, creating self-destructing call option
Youth-bulge-instability correlation (Urdal 2006): governance is the mediating variable
Conditional probability of young-population EM generating above-median USD equity returns: 35-45%
Fertility and income negatively correlated above $8K-12K GDP per capita
open_questions: Which current EM countries have institutional quality sufficient to realize the dividend?
How quickly does the dividend self-destruct once income growth takes hold?
Is the $8K-12K GDP per capita fertility threshold shifting?
